summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of an Oct 1980 show, highlighted by a stellar acoustic set and standout Deal/Stranger performance.
review: The recording delivers a clear, enjoyable soundboard experience – not as immaculate as some Betty tapes but still very listenable. Musically the concert shines with an exceptional acoustic set, a beautiful rendition of “To Lay Me Down,” and a memorable Deal > Stranger sequence, plus a whisper‑soft “Eyes of the World.” Overall the show is considered one of the more fun October‑80 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently strong praise for both performance and audio quality across most reviews; only minor recording complaints and one outlier spam review.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and crisp; a few listeners note it isn’t as pristine as other Betty recordings, but the overall mix is solid and very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The acoustic set is repeatedly praised as a highlight, and the second set maintains that energy with strong song choices like Deal > Stranger; overall pacing keeps the show fun and engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar on “Eyes of the World” is described as whisper‑soft and beautiful.
